Paul Tosh presents  ◆  A Premium Solo Show  ◆  tosh42.com

Paper
Gun

This is mine, but you can have it.

A man. A piano. A reckoning.

EMOTIONALLY ENGAGING  ·  THEATRICAL  ·  LIBERATED

The Work

Paper Gun is an original song cycle that begins in warmth and ends in freedom — and takes no prisoners on the way.

This work is personal and cathartic — but in delivery the perspective broadens. I find myself more careful for the audience's sake than I am for my own. I still want to be honest, and inspire — but it is the privilege of doing so, getting to explore ideas in a way that makes a connection, that you come to know yourself better. It is an alchemy that feels like it comes from above.

These songs were written across two decades. They are true.

The Programme

Each performance weaves together original composition, literary adaptation, dramatic storytelling, words of wisdom, and communal song into a single unbroken arc. Nothing is incidental. Every choice serves the theme.

What People Say

"I especially like the substantive intellectual meaning that you weave into your music. You remind me of the inspiring Shlomo Carlebach ע״ש."
Rabbi Daniel Lapin — RabbiDanielLapin.com
"Last night made me feel like the pre-WWII era of the arts in society! Loved it."
Libby Fox (hostess) — Charleston home concert — swampfoxdockandmarine.com
"It's Beatles, it's U2, it's Bob Dylan, Lenny Kravitz and Billy Joel, it's jazz — you write this style and that style — you have obviously been influenced by all the songs you have been playing through the years. The world would welcome something like this now with open arms. The songs are timeless. It's an album like Sgt. Pepper."
Orna Wellman — Drama and English teacher, Canada — instagram.com/ornawellman
"Really good solid organic original material — it has to be experienced live. They're not simple in terms of structure nor in terms of emotional content."
Miguel Cavazos — Session drummer, LA
"I can't believe the level of artistry you're bringing to the people in these residential homes. I bet they'd never seen anything like it before."
Joe Tepperman — Author & theater writer
"Light, serious, fun. Paul's songs are alive — you have to live with them. Paul also sets the standard when it comes to camaraderie and healthy artistic sharing."
Mesoud Benasuly — Session bass player, New York City
"I think I've listened to that song a hundred times — but that might be the first time I've actually heard it."
Video testimonial — audience member
"A poetic call to presence — reminding us that the pursuit itself is the purpose, and that love, more than anything, is the foundation of it all. So much fun to watch Paul's creative brain at work… a true pleasure."
Amy Kirshtein — at Libby & Daniel's home concert
"This is as different a performance as anything I've seen in the Listening Room Network. It's really more performance art than musical performance — as much poetry as it is music. His piano playing is just exceptional."
Listening Room Network — Audition Review, August 2025

From the audience —

"When people don't get up, you know we don't want to leave."
"You made me cry several times. I wasn't planning to."
"Even as the performance ended and we instinctively returned to lighthearted conversation, the depth of what we had experienced lingered within each of us, waiting to be unpacked in solitude. Unforgettable."
"You love what you're doing, and that love comes out to all of us."
"You are amongst those that keep the world alive."
"I like to get into your mode though. Because I haven't heard music like this before. And you're free — you know some people stick to things, but you're very fluid."

For Whom & Where

The Right Room Matters

This is not background music. It is not a dinner show. It is a performance that asks something of its audience — and gives a great deal more in return.

The Audience

  • Those with cultural depth and patience for emotional arc
  • Appreciators of literary and dramatic work over production spectacle
  • The genuinely musically literate
  • Those who are emotionally present and not irony-armoured
  • People who value presence and authenticity over celebrity

The Venues

  • Small theatres (50–150 seats)
  • Art galleries and literary events
  • Thoughtful fundraisers
  • Private salon performances
  • College and university arts programmes
  • Retreats and conferences
  • Supper clubs
  • Hotel special engagements
  • Cruise special shows
  • Piano shops and showrooms
  • Shuls and church halls
  • Community centres
  • Festivals

Investment

$2,500 Discounts available — circumstances considered.

All-inclusive · Travel · Accommodation · Sound

Paul travels in a custom self-contained performance camper, making him available for venues across the entire United States and Canada — with no hotel riders, no staging requirements, and no hidden costs.

About the Performer

Paul Tosh

P

Born in Scotland. Formed in Europe. Seasoned across a decade of performances for senior communities on the American East Coast. Paul Tosh is a therapeutic musical performer whose work refuses the easy distinction between entertainment and art.

His signature approach — Song Is The Play — treats every performance as a piece of theatre: looping piano, dramatic song, and the kind of presence that makes a familiar melody feel heard for the first time. Paper Gun is his most personal and fully realised work to date: a show about damage, clarity, and the quiet violence of the truth.

These songs were written across two decades. They are true.

THE SONG IS THE PLAY

Song Is The Play

Paul's celebrated concert show — Billy Joel, The Beatles, Bob Dylan, Sinatra, Neil Diamond, and originals, performed as theatrical prose with looping piano.

Video Testimonials

Hear It From the Audience

The Programme

Twenty Songs. One Reckoning.

Click any song to read a line.

Act One  ·  The Trap

1.Beautiful SunshineThe opening›

"Your life is amazing — you still have some left."

2.The VisitorsThe shadow arrives›

"Your voice has got that colour, the sound of 'I don't care.'"

3.From a Watcher's Point of ViewThe narcissist observed›

"He agrees with everything you do. But you've got to admire him for being so shrewd."

4.Who Knows Just WhyThe protagonist questions himself›

"Who knows just why we turned around and hide when reminded of love?"

5.Paper GunThe weapon named›

"I've justified most every lie. Morality can feign."

6.Don't Say AnythingThe silence and isolation›

"I was so generous on stage, but in private I couldn't give anything away."

7.Fear HateThe universal warning›

"One of life's hardest things is not to allow fear to turn to hate."

Act Two  ·  The Struggle

8.PressuresThe weight of it›

"Value is not defined by vocation, by season, or even a kiss."

9.Bully the BullyFighting back›

"I know in this song the bully is me, but the bully is feeling free."

10.ForgivingThe impossible ask›

"A bit faster than mountains, but slower than clouds. Contradiction's the terms — it's all that's allowed."

11.LemonSourness, deflection, raw confrontation›

"When I'm raw from something I saw — to keep me alive I kill it inside."

12.The Space BetweenThe prayer in the eye of the storm›

"The space above is filled with love, but the space between is what gets you there."

13.Trauma ResponseThe most raw and honest piece in the collection›

"When we both are gone, please G-d we'll get along."

14.A Little AmnestyThe plea›

"Whatever the scheme, in the lines or in between — try to broker a little amnesty."

15.Broken Man (optional)The raw plea›

"Don't test me or arrest me, but don't second guess me — bless me."

Act Three  ·  The Emergence

16.This Is MineReclamation›

"This is mine, but you can have it — 'cause it's all I've got to give."

17.You and Your OwnUnderstanding the pattern›

"We always hurt the ones we love — it's not just you, everyone."

18.Paper CrownThe turning point›

"If you had faith you'd feel secure, you wouldn't need control, then you could be vulnerable, then authentic, then intimate."

19.Let Me Cry (optional)The release›

"With our tears we'd find a way — we'd recognise what we want to say."

20.Truthful LoveThe arrival›

"Are you in the mood to sacrifice truth for love?"

21.Painful BotherThe cost of freedom›

"As much as you are inseparable from your nose and thumb."

22.FaithThe resolution›

"If you've seen the good, you'll see the best."

23.By HertzFaith applied›

"For if the road you have chosen is just — one also travels in trust."

24.Beautiful Sunshine (Reprise)The return›

"You make me rock n roll."
